108.status: Exited May 1 start after taking a 102.6 mph liner off the outside of his right knee. Was removed as a precaution and believes he can make his next start.
Left his outing before the start of the third inning on April 29. Underwent an MRI exam and is awaiting the results.
Began a rehab assignment with Single-A Rancho Cucamonga on April 29 by throwing a scoreless inning with a strikeout. Will make another appearance on May 2.
Continuing to build back up and has had no complications during rehab assignment, which will resume with Single-A Rancho Cucamonga on May 2. Still needs to improve his velocity.
Right middle fingernail fell off after March 17 start. Threw four simulated innings to hitters on April 21 and five more on April 27. Begins rehab assignment with Single-A Rancho Cucamonga on May 2.
Threw on April 24 in Arizona and felt good. Progressed to throwing four simulated innings and 60 pitches on April 30.
Was removed from April 25 game after taking a foul tip off his left wrist. Testing revealed the fracture, but he said it was minor and doesn't expect to be out too long.

Made a rehab start in a Minor League game in Arizona but exited with hamstring discomfort after throwing two pitches on April 13. He threw bullpens without any issues before progressing to facing hitters in an extended spring training game on April 27.
Was evaluated by Dr. Keith Meister in Texas and underwent season-ending ligament and flexor tendon repair surgery, the club announced April 8.
Not going to play in 2026 and had the last year of his contract deferred over several years. Was placed on 60-day IL on March 24.

110.status: Placed on IL prior to opener of four-game series at Yankee Stadium. Imaging came back clean. Taking anti-inflammatory medication. Not expected to be a long-term issue.
Dealing with a flu that has circulated around Orioles' clubhouse. Should be ready to go at end of IL stint.

Exited first inning of new rehab assignment with Triple-A Norfolk (his 14th overall rehab game) due to right hand discomfort on April 21. MRI, CT scans, X-rays all clean. Expects to return to rehab games week of May 4.
Injury was sustained in days after April 18 start in Cleveland. Expected to miss at least several weeks.
Sustained injury during March 19 Grapefruit League game. Underwent imaging on March 23 that revealed low-grade strain. Doing full baseball activities in Sarasota, Fla., as of April 24.
After testing, received platelet-rich plasma injection on Feb. 20. Continues to hit and throw in Sarasota, Fla., as of April 24.
Got second opinion with Dr. Neal ElAttrache in Los Angeles on April 20. Has inflammation in shoulder joint. Felt soreness coming out of March 31 outing.
Exited April 11 game vs. Giants after stumbling between first and second and sliding awkwardly into the bag on a double. Broke fourth metatarsal on left foot. Expected to miss substantial time.
Exited 2026 debut on March 31 due to right elbow discomfort. After getting imaging and a second opinion, underwent Tommy John surgery on April 8.
Underwent surgery to repair torn rotator cuff and torn labrum last Aug. 19. Threw for first time March 3, tossing 25 pitches from 45 feet on flat ground.
Placed on the 60-day injured list on Feb. 14. No clear timeline for potential return.

111.status: Threw live BP on May 1 and will have conversations to decide a date for him to rejoin the rotation.
MRI came back clean, but he will not throw for a few days. Expects to be able to return when his IL stint is up.
Nearing rehab assignment, potentially this weekend in Somerset with Double-A Portland.
Underwent additional testing after being recalled from his Minor League rehab assignment on April 20, and imaging revealed nothing alarming.

111.injury: Ruptured left patellar tendon/left abdominal strain
111.status: Rehab from left knee surgery went well, but now has left abdominal strain that will prolong return to action.
Imaging on right elbow revealed no structural damage. Waiting for flexor strain irritation to lessen before he can resume throwing.
Consultation with Dr. Keith Meister revealed right flexor strain. Shut down from throwing for six weeks. Transferred to 60-day IL on April 14.

111.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament right elbow/right flexor pronator strain
111.status: Started gradual throwing program on flat ground in the second week of Spring Training.

112.status: Held out of lineup May 2 due to lingering discomfort. Tightness flared pregame May 1 and Hoerner exited after the top of the second vs. D-backs.
Averaged 100.4 mph (max velo 102 mph) on 13 fastballs in 19-pitch rehab outing (two-thirds of an inning) with Triple-A Iowa on May 1. Could be activated in next few days.
Removed in ninth inning against Phillies on April 23. Has been throwing, and Cubs hope he will be able to return close to eligibility date.

Felt discomfort in recent bullpen session. Checkup with Dr. Keith Meister (who did Steele's UCL revision repair on April 18, 2025) showed a flexor strain. Shut down from throwing until asymptomatic, and will be rechecked in a month.
Injured hand while working out April 13. Had rehab outings for Triple-A Iowa on April 25 and April 28. Next step to be determined.
Threw 57 pitches in two innings (six runs allowed) in third rehab outing with Triple-A Iowa on April 28.
Felt some discomfort in April 23 outing and did not recover well. Further testing showed a flexor strain; timetable estimated to be around eight weeks.

112.playerName: Jaxon Wiggins (Cubs' No. 1 prospect, No. 50 overall)
112.status: Last pitched on April 4 for Iowa. Moved to Minor League IL out of caution. Has resumed playing catch, per president of baseball operations Jed Hoyer.
Has resumed a throwing program, but still has several steps in his build back to pitching.
Underwent reconstruction of ulnar collateral ligament with an internal brace April 20 via Dr. Keith Meister. Initial timetable for return is 12-14 months.
Underwent a right UCL revision repair with an internal brace on April 16. Timetable for return is estimated around 15-16 months.
Underwent a right patellar tendon debridement on Feb. 24. Could miss months while recovering.
Underwent surgery to repair a torn right UCL and flexor tendon last October. Signed a two-year deal with Chicago on Feb. 16 with an eye toward contributing in 2027.

113.status: MRI showed no structural issues and Williamson received a cortisone injection. He exited his April 29 start after three innings due to the issue.
Hopes to travel with team on road trip through Pittsburgh and Chicago and get another scan to see where he stands in healing process. Has been running to stay in shape.
Threw 14 pitches (seven strikes) at Double-A Chattanooga on April 28. Will throw again May 1 as an opener before throwing out of the bullpen next week on rehab.
Has begun a throwing program, and his first bullpen session could happen in late May. Greene had arthroscopic surgery March 11 to remove bone chips and loose bodies from elbow.
Threw 51 pitches, including six sliders, over five scoreless innings on a rehab assignment with High-A Dayton on April 26. Up next is a rehab start for Triple-A Louisville on May 2.

114.status: Felt issue while warming up in bullpen on April 24. Expected to be a minimum IL stint. Eligible to return on May 8.
Made sixth appearance of rehab assignment on April 24 with Triple-A Columbus
Exited early from Triple-A Columbus' game on April 22 due to issue. Issue considered mild.
Suffered injury running to second base while hitting a double in fifth inning on April 6. Imaging revealed moderate strain. Expected to miss four to eight weeks.

115.status: Played catch May 1, his second time doing so since his most recent appearance on April 23. Plan is to schedule a bullpen session.
Allowed two runs on three hits with two strikeouts in one inning for High-A Spokane in his first injury rehab appearance on April 29.

115.expectedReturn: To be determined after he is cleared for game action
115.status: Continues playing catch at the team's facility in Scottsdale, Ariz.

Underwent ligament transfer Tommy John surgery and internal brace procedure on March 4.
There is no return in sight for Bryant, who has had a long and painful time since signing with the Rockies before the 2022 season.

116.status: Will begin a rehab assignment with Single-A Lakeland on May 3.
Transferred to Triple-A Toledo for next stage of his rehab assignment.
Scheduled to throw another bullpen session this weekend.

Threw his second live session to hitters on May 1.
Playing catch and completing rehab daily.
Sent to Single-A Lakeland on rehab assignment on April 30.
Exited his April 28 start in the third inning. Reported gradual discomfort that did not improve between innings. The injury was diagnosed as a Grade 1 right adductor strain.
Completing rehab daily after receiving a cortisone injection in mid-April.

117.status: Will make his second Minor League start on May 5 or 6 for Triple-A Sugar Land, after which he's expected to rejoin the Houston rotation.
Joined Double-A Corpus Christi for a Minor League rehab assignment on May 1, playing shortstop and hitting second.
Worked out with the team at Fenway Park on May 1 ahead of starting a rehab assignment.
Has started his throwing program by playing catch up to 120 feet.
Threw a live BP on April 28, including against Jeremy Peña. He'll throw one more live BP before heading out on rehab.
Threw two innings in a Minor League rehab assignment April 23 for Triple-A Sugar Land, allowing one hit, four walks and one run while striking out three on 31 strikes out of 53 pitches.
Should start a throwing program soon after exiting his April 8 start against Rockies with right shoulder tightness.
Performing light baseball activities as he prepares to start a running program.
Has begun light baseball activity after exiting the game against the Rockies on April 8 mid-plate appearance following a swing.
Exited after running the bases vs. the Guardians on April 20. Manager Joe Espada said it will "take some time for him to get back."

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in right elbow
117.status: Has progressed to throwing in the bullpen in West Palm Beach after undergoing Tommy John surgery to repair the ulnar collateral ligament in his right elbow on May 23, 2025.

117.injury: Torn ulnar collateral ligament in left elbow
117.status: Underwent Tommy John surgery on Sept. 23, 2025.

Threw 72 pitches in 4 1/3 innings in his fourth rehab start with Triple-A Omaha on April 29, striking out four and allowing eight hits and three runs (one earned).
Struck out two in 1 1/3 innings in his fifth rehab appearance on April 29. Slated to pitch again May 2.
Underwent left labral repair surgery on April 28, ending his season.
After missing 2025 season with an elbow injury, has continued to have problems with soreness. Scheduled to see a specialist to determine next steps.
After missing 2025 with shoulder issues, underwent right labral surgery in October. Rehabbing in Surprise, Ariz., during Spring Training but likely out for year.

119.status: Stretched out to 39 pitches (29 strikes) across three scoreless innings in his second rehab start with Single-A Ontario on April 28. Scheduled to start for Triple-A Oklahoma City on May 3.
Reached a milestone by pitching in back-to-back games with Triple-A Oklahoma City from April 29-30. Doing an up-down is likely the last box he needs to check on his rehab assignment.
Tweaked his side while throwing in the bullpen on March 13. He will not begin a throwing program until he's pain-free.
Had surgery on his elbow on April 22. He thinks he will be able to start playing catch a couple weeks after the stitches are removed from his elbow.
Taking batting practice in the cage. Betts is getting closer to beginning a rehab assignment.
Hitting upper 90s in his bullpen sessions at the Dodgers' complex in Arizona, per manager Dave Roberts.
Felt discomfort in his shoulder after his outing on April 12.
Imaging revealed inflammation, but not structural damage, in his shoulder. Has started a throwing program and essentially has an entire Spring Training ramp-up ahead.
Underwent Tommy John surgery in June 2025 and could be a second-half option for the Dodgers.
Progressed to throwing off the mound by the end of Spring Training, but the team is building him up slowly.
Started throwing full bullpen sessions in mid-March.

120.status: MRI results revealed there is no structural damage, will work through throwing progression.
Began plyometrics program. Nats exercising caution because Gray is returning from Tommy John surgery.

120.playerName: Jarlin Susana (Nationals' No. 4 prospect, MLB No. 70)

120.playerName: Travis Sykora (Nationals' No. 2 prospect, MLB No. 48)
120.status: Continuing throwing progression, including throwing on flat ground three times a week.

121.status: Wrist soreness popped up April 14 in addition to his Achilles discomfort. Has been performing light baseball activities, including batting practice on the field on May 1, but remains in a week-to-week progression.
Robert received an injection April 30 and will rest for seven to 10 days. He will be re-evaluated then to determine his potential return date.

Began a rehab assignment April 9 with Single-A St. Lucie, putting him on track to return May 9 at the latest. Is scheduled to pitch for Triple-A Syracuse on May 1 and 3 and should be activated toward the end of that week.
Began experiencing back discomfort following his April 26 start. Received an epidural. Will not throw for 7-10 days, after which point the Mets will re-evaluate.
Strained calf April 22. Will be in a boot for a week and undergo a follow-up MRI the week of May 11. Will remain on a fluid timeline until he receives results.
Began feeling knee discomfort during an April 12 game against the Athletics. An April 14 MRI revealed a meniscus tear, requiring surgery. Had stitches removed the week of April 20.

Developed a blister during an April 13 appearance in Los Angeles. Shouldn't need more than the minimum 15 days to return, though his roster spot will depend on whether the Mets have a need once he's ready.
Exited the Mets' March 21 game after four innings due to knee discomfort and underwent an MRI, which revealed the tear. Will spend his recovery on the Minor League IL.
Fractured a rib during a March 5 game against the Nationals. Will need 4-8 weeks to recover, after which he can commence his buildup.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2027.
Will miss all of 2026 recovering from surgery but is under team control through 2029.

133.status: Exited his start on April 30 following the third inning after feeling discomfort in his hip. Scheduled to make his next start on May 6 in Philadelphia.

133.injury: Mid foot bone bruise in right foot
133.status: Expected to begin a running progression during the week of April 27. Hitting progression would likely come after that.
Began a throwing progression April 19. No timetable for his return.

134.status: Will make next rehab start on May 5 with Triple-A Indianapolis, after striking out five batters and reaching 101 mph in three innings in April 29 rehab start with Single-A Bradenton. Plans to rotate starts between Indianapolis and Double-A Altoona until he is ready to rejoin the big league club.
Will play in back-to-back games for the first time on April 30 and May 1. Went 3-for-4 with a home run, three RBIs and two runs scored in his first game with Double-A Altoona on April 28.

135.status: Has made five rehab appearances with Triple-A El Paso, including five innings of one-run ball on April 28. Scheduled for at least one more rehab start, but could return afterward.
Only appeared in five Cactus League games after sustaining the injury in early March. Began a rehab assignment with Triple-A El Paso on May 1.
Missed World Baseball Classic due to the injury, then began the season on the IL. Began rehab stint with Triple-A El Paso April 4 and could be nearing a return.
Underwent surgery in October 2024. Was hopeful for an early season return, but experienced a setback after making one start in Spring Training. Playing light catch but has yet to throw off the mound.

Exited April 12 start with elbow stiffness; shut down from throwing. MRI revealed flexor strain. Expected to pitch again this season, though manager Craig Stammen noted Pivetta's absence would last "weeks and maybe months."
Underwent surgery to repair the flexor tendon in his right elbow on March 17 and will miss the 2026 season.

Underwent an internal brace procedure on his right elbow on Oct. 29, which comes with a recovery timetable of 12-15 months. Will miss the 2026 season and was placed on the restricted list on March 25.
Underwent UCL internal brace surgery as well as a flexor tendon repair operation in April 2025. Resumed throwing bullpens in Padres camp.

136.status: Will not throw for 3-5 days then slowly build back up. MRI revealed "trace inflammation" but no structural damage.More »
Slated for rehab assignment at Double-A Arkansas from May 5-6 then will be activated on May 8 at White Sox at start of next road trip, if all goes well.
Will make fourth rehab start on May 6 at High-A Everett, in range of 65-70 pitches. Club will assess his status after, and whether he needs a fifth outing or is ready for activation.
Slated to begin rehab assignment at Triple-A Tacoma on May 3, which will at minimum last seven days. Final hurdle to clear is throwing to the plate, along with live at-bats.
Injured the other (left) groin during a rehab assignment and was shut down on April 18. Will be reassessed in mid-to-late May to begin a fresh rehab assignment.
Experienced setback during early stages of throwing program and will now have to begin fresh once he's cleared to throw again.
Slated to begin rehab assignment at Triple-A Tacoma the weekend of May 2-3. Suffered the injury during brief callup from April 14-16 in San Diego.
Underwent surgery to repair his right ulnar collateral ligament in January, and will be out all season.More »

137.status: Has logged a 6.14 ERA over 7 1/3 innings in his first eight rehab appearances with Triple-A Sacramento.
Expected to miss six to eight weeks after undergoing surgery to remove a broken hamate from his wrist on April 16. Transferred to the 60-day IL on April 29.
Has started his hitting program and is expected to resume throwing later this week.
Started a baserunning progression on April 28.
Has given up two runs over 4 2/3 innings in his first five rehab appearances with Triple-A Sacramento.
Underwent surgery to repair the anterior capsule in his right shoulder in May 2025. Threw a live batting practice session in Arizona on April 28.
Underwent a second surgery to repair an axillary artery in his upper right arm on April 17, which will sideline him for five to six months.
Began a throwing program on April 6 and is currently tossing bullpens in Arizona.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with Dr. Keith Meister on March 25.
Expected to miss 12-14 months after undergoing Tommy John surgery on Sept. 24, 2025. Was slated to begin a throwing progression in mid-March.
Expected to miss the entire 2026 season after undergoing Tommy John surgery in November 2025.

138.status: Pitched for the second straight day for Triple-A Memphis on April 30, throwing a scoreless inning vs. Indianapolis.
Underwent surgery last October to address Haglund’s deformities in both heels. Started his running progression on March 20, but no date has been set for a return to the lineup.

139.status: Pitched one inning April 26 for Triple-A Durham, then recorded two outs in back-to-back appearances April 29-30 to complete his rehab assignment. Will rejoin Rays soon.

Threw a 25-pitch bullpen on April 12, then received an injection in his hip while being transferred to the 60-day IL. Felt "unstable" playing catch from 60 feet on April 20. Set for season-ending surgery on May 13.

Imaging revealed no structural damage, and manager Kevin Cash called the IL move cautionary. Faced hitters in live BP on April 30. Will throw a bullpen on May 3 then potentially begin rehab assignment.
Imaging revealed no serious injury. Faced hitters in live BP on April 30. Will throw a bullpen on May 3 then potentially begin rehab assignment. Will be built back up to cover multiple innings.

139.status: Sprained ankle running to first base April 11 on rehab assignment. Resumed assignment with Triple-A Durham on April 24. Has been playing regularly, but still feeling some stiffness in his ankle.
Sustained a Grade 2 left ankle sprain while running the bases for Triple-A Durham on April 22. Imaging revealed no fracture.

After rocky rehab outing on April 15, he reported soreness in back of right shoulder. Further evaluation revealed a new injury, a subscapularis strain. Shut down for 2-3 weeks, meaning he's likely 4-6 weeks from returning.
Signed to a Major League contract on April 14 and immediately placed on the IL. Was about three weeks away from facing hitters at the time.
Underwent surgery to repair a right elbow flexor strain on Aug. 8. Hopes to return in June or July.
Did not pitch all spring while dealing with a back issue that required an injection. Will need a while to build up after being shut down so long.

140.status: Langford removed himself from the game May 1 at the team complex in Arizona after feeling something “on a swing and miss,” according to manager Skip Schumaker. The club is working to get him live at-bats, and he will be re-evaluated.

Had another setback in throwing program. Will continue to be no-throw for at least another few days. Hopes to throw plyo balls early next week. Threw 27 pitches in rehab appearance with Triple-A Round Rock April 2. Has not pitched since.
Was up to 97 mph in one inning of a rehab appearance with Triple-A Round Rock on April 23. Will need to throw on back-to-back days before he returns to the big league club.
Examined by team doctors on April 20 for shoulder soreness and was placed on the IL on April 23.
Only threw one inning in an April 14 loss to the A’s due to arm fatigue that was eventually classified as shoulder impingement.
Left Tuesday night’s loss to the A’s after throwing two pitches due to feeling something in his arm. Scans showed minimal issues, though he will be down for at least two weeks.
Down four to six weeks minimum before re-evaluation.
Signed to a Major League deal on Feb. 13, though is continuing to rehab from Tommy John surgery. Should be back on a big league mound around the Trade Deadline.

141.status: Will make one more rehab start in Triple-A Buffalo to increase his pitch count, then will likely return to the Blue Jays.
Has started throwing. Next steps include catching and hitting, but Kirk is progressing on schedule.
Beginning rehab process at Florida complex and remains week to week.

141.status: No further testing or anti-inflammatories needed. Will shut down for 5-7 days and reevaluate.

In early stages of his own "Spring Training" ramp-up and will continue to face live hitters.
Remains in Florida. Manager John Schneider did not have an immediate update on his throwing status but mentioned he would be checking on it.
Discovered after Estrada was optioned, so he has been placed on the Major League IL.
Underwent surgery and is expected to miss the rest of 2026.
Underwent surgery to repair the labral tear and is hoping to return to the Blue Jays in five to six months. This timeline should be clearer by June, but any return in 2026 would be a success.
Underwent surgery and will miss the entire 2026 season.

142.status: Threw a bullpen session Saturday and was encouraged. Next steps TBD, but is progressing well.

144.status: Slated to make his season debut on May 3.

Could be available at some point after the All-Star break.

Making progress and getting better every day, as he told MLB.com on April 29. But there’s still no set timetable for an injury rehab assignment.

Scheduled to begin an injury rehab assignment with Triple-A Charlotte on April 25.
Underwent season-ending Tommy John surgery with the procedure performed by Dr. Keith Meister, but was with the team for the home opener.

Underwent successful surgery to repair the torn ACL on Oct. 28. Estimated recovery time is eight months, per the team.
Continues with the bullpen portion of his recovery ramp-up, having thrown seven. Has felt great as the rehab process moves forward.

146.status: Scheduled to play catch on May 2. Exited April 27 outing after experiencing diminished sensation in pitching hand.

147.status: Made third rehab start on April 29, throwing 5 2/3 innings for Double-A Somerset. Scheduled to make fourth rehab start on May 5 for High-A Hudson valley.
Hit by pitch April 29. Had CT scan taken in New York on April 30 and results were negative.
Told Yankees he is ready to rejoin team, but will continue playing with Double-A Somerset. Club will re-evaluate plans for him on May 3.
Made second rehab start on April 30 for Double-A Somerset, throwing 76 pitches (52 strikes) over 5 1/3 scoreless innings. Will make at least one more rehab start before club determines next steps.

Will not throw for approximately three weeks. Manager Aaron Boone said there is “acute and chronic stuff going on there” that will require rest. Experienced difficulty while throwing on April 25 in Houston.

Threw 10 pitches from a mound April 10, his first time doing so since Tommy John surgery in July 2025. Could face hitters in June and is on track for MLB activation sometime in August.

Exited his April 30 start against the D-backs after 1 1/3 innings because of diminished velocity. MRI revealed shoulder inflammation but no structural damage. (last updated: May 1)
Weighing his options after additional testing and a second opinion. "Surgery is an option," per manager Pat Murphy. (last updated: May 1)
Began a rehab assignment at Triple-A Nashville on April 29. Has gone 1-for-4 with three walks in two games. (last updated: May 1)
Began a rehab assignment at Triple-A Nashville on April 29. Has gone 2-for-6 with a double and a walk in two games. (last updated: May 1)
Ramping up hitting/running progression as of April 28 and is hitting flips and off a tee. (last updated: April 28)
Began a throwing program on April 28. (last updated: April 28)
Scheduled for four innings or 60-65 pitches in his next rehab start on May 1. (last updated: April 28)
Returned from rehab assignment on April 4 after suffering a ribcage injury while coming back from a shoulder injury. Was playing catch at 90 feet as of April 24. (last updated: April 28)
Injured running to first base on March 9, and an MRI revealed a more serious injury than initially believed. (last updated: March 25)
